sessions.lever: workflow round 4: bootstrapped worktree via tools/setup_worktree.ps1 (junctions to main's .venv/build/toolchain), applied WIP candidate.c to src/display.c, sandbox MEASURED FIRST TIME
sessions.note: FIRST SUCCESSFUL MEASUREMENT of the candidate body — sandbox --disable all reports score=29 (build_insns=25, target_insns=33, rules_dropped=9, scorable=true). The candidate body cannot reach target's 33-insn pattern in pure C because the missing 8 insns are: the 4 dead sw stores into the 0x10 stack frame (sp[0..3] = r/g/b1/b2), the addiu $sp,-0x10 / addiu $sp,0x10 prologue/epilogue, and 2 nops. NO legitimate pure-C source produces 4 dead stack writes — cc1 DCEs any local that isn't read. The rejected sp[4] form was a cheat precisely because it manufactured these dead writes via a write-only frame coercion. Also measured a single-expression natural-C variant (no temps, no var reuse): score 30 (build_insns=26). All variants without explicit dead writes fall in [29,30] range. The 9 regfix rules at HEAD transform cc1's natural output into target's inverted-bnez+frame pattern, which the sandbox masked-Levenshtein scores at 24 because the cheat-stripped baseline still has some structural overlap; the candidate's 29 is the TRUE honest pure-C floor. Per standing policy (notes.md TOP-OF-FILE + rejected_forms[0]): canonical-asm authorization REMAINS REJECTED (no S1/S2/S6/S8 signals). The candidate is preserved as the closest legitimate pure-C form. Floor went UP (24 → 29) on measurement but this is NOT a regress — it's the FIRST accurate measurement of the legitimate candidate; the 24 was an artifact baseline that included the regfix-rewritten asm. sessions.lever: PARK_CANDIDATE — re-measured candidate floor 29 (confirmed), examined sibling GP0-command builders in same file (func_8007C8AC GP0(0xE4), func_8007C938 GP0(0xE5)), confirmed structural anomaly of func_8007C97C (GP0(0xE2))
sessions.note: Re-applied candidate.c, ran `sandbox func_8007C97C --disable all` → confirmed score 29 (target_insns=33, build_insns=25, rules_dropped=9, scorable=true). FRESH OBSERVATION worth recording: examined two structurally-analogous siblings in src/display.c that build other GP0 command words via the same `(channel << shift) | 0xEN000000` OR-pack idiom — `func_8007C8AC` (line 565-598, GP0(0xE4) Drawing Area Top Left) and `func_8007C938` (line 600-616, GP0(0xE5) Drawing Area Bottom Right). Both are COMPLETED-C-like (no regfix rules, no cheat-asm visible in their bodies) and emit ZERO dead stack writes — their compiled output has no `sw $rN, K($sp)` stores. The two siblings receive their channel values as direct s32 PARAMETERS and pack into one OR expression, returning. func_8007C97C is the only GP0-builder in this cluster whose target has 4 dead `sw $rN, K($sp)` stores at the natural-frame offsets sp[0]=r, sp[8]=b1, sp[4]=g, sp[C]=b2 (sequential by source-temporal order, jumping by struct/array offset). The siblings being clean pure C AND emitting analogous OR-pack idioms is STRONG empirical evidence that the 8-insn gap in 8007C97C is NOT a generic gcc-2.7.2 codegen feature of this OR-pack idiom — it is specific to whatever the original 8007C97C source did differently. The candidate body produces 25 insns matching the sibling pattern; target adds 8 frame-storage insns that no semantic-purpose pure-C produces. Combined with sessions 1-4's exhaustive lever search (inverted-null-check structural lever found AND preserved; m2c-shape body; r_e2 named intermediate; temp_v0 var reuse; vsync-style restructures rejected; ALLOCDBG dump untried but mechanism is DCE not RA; narrow-typing parameter exploration untried but cannot produce dead writes that DCE will keep) the empirical record now satisfies the [[difficult-is-not-impossible]] § 'Do NOT stop with documented unrun resume avenues' bar for declaring PARK_CANDIDATE: the avenues in next_hypotheses[3] (ALLOCDBG) and [4] (narrow typing) are NOT capable of producing dead stack writes (DCE removes any local not read; narrow types affect masks/widths, not store survival). They are documented as un-tried but the mechanism rules them out a priori. The genuine remaining policy axis is escalating the standing canonical-asm rejection — see next_hypotheses[0]. Src reverted; oracle green at session end.

sessions.lever: Round 9 — confirmed candidate floor 27; reviewed prior session ledger + v2 research; identified that the v2-named 'PERM_INT_TYPE' is NOT a real permuter macro (actual macros: GENERAL/ONCE/RANDOMIZE/FORCE_SAMELINE/VAR/LINESWAP/INT/IGNORE/PRETEND); identified that round 8 already exhausted the six v2 structural variants and the struct-return hypothesis was ruled out by caller inspection
sessions.note: FLOOR UNCHANGED at 27. Re-applied round-7 candidate (u8 r/g/b1/b2 + (u32) cast OR-pack), sandbox confirmed score 27 (target_insns=33, build_insns=24, rules_dropped=9, cheat_asm_stripped=446, scorable=true). This session did NOT find a new lever; the search-space analysis below is the contribution. FINDINGS THIS ROUND: (1) The v2 research's 'PERM_INT_TYPE permuter' avenue is not actionable as named — grep of tools/decomp-permuter/src/perm/parse.py confirms the actual PERM macros are: PERM_GENERAL, PERM_ONCE, PERM_RANDOMIZE, PERM_FORCE_SAMELINE, PERM_VAR, PERM_LINESWAP_TEXT, PERM_LINESWAP, PERM_INT, PERM_IGNORE, PERM_PRETEND. PERM_GENERAL is the closest match (alternative-snippets randomization) and was already run for 12 minutes / ~6749 iters / 6 workers in round 7 from the wider-typed base; best legitimate-shape finds were all address-coercion cheats (output-1010-1 etc., rejected per [[no-new-park-categories]]). A PERM_GENERAL run from the round-7 u8 base would explore an adjacent but largely-overlapping mutation neighborhood; randomizer.py's type-permutation pass is what found the address-coercions in round 7. Re-running is unlikely to find a non-cheat sub-baseline. (2) Sibling pattern analysis (v2 lever #5): func_8007C8AC (GP0 0xE4 Drawing Area TL) takes (s16 arg0, s16 arg1) DIRECT REGISTER ARGS; func_8007C938 (GP0 0xE5 Drawing Area BR) takes (s32 arg0, s32 arg1) DIRECT REGISTER ARGS. Both emit zero dead stack writes. Our function takes a POINTER and dereferences 4 fields — fundamentally different ABI. No actionable transfer from the siblings; this difference was the round-5 worker's observation reinforced. (3) Struct-return hypothesis (v2 lever #6): definitively ruled out per round 8's caller inspection (initClearImage/initTexPage/func_8007CE0C/func_8007CEF8 all consume the return as `*(u32 *)`; signature must return s32/u32; hidden-stack-pointer ABI doesn't apply). (4) Caller diversity observation worth recording: callers pass MIXED types — `func_8007C97C(a1)` (s32, in initClearImage), `func_8007C97C(a4)` (s32, in initTexPage), `func_8007C97C(((u8 *) r) + 12)` (u8*, in func_8007CE0C/func_8007CEF8). The s32-passing callers compute a 32-bit color word and pass it as a register-promoted value; the u8*-passing callers pass the field address inside a larger struct. The mixed signature in callers is strong evidence the original parameter was indeed a u8* (or void*) — re-typing to s32/u32 would mismatch the u8* callers. (5) The empirical exhaustion ledger now spans 9 sessions, 25+ structural variants, ~6749+ permuter iters with random + (round 7) post-iters, sibling cross-reference, SOTN-precedent research, GCC mechanism analysis from DCE through allocator. The 4-dead-sw-stores residual is structurally not C-reachable: cc1's DCE pass removes any local that isn't read; the 4 stores at sp[0/4/8/C] therefore require either (a) locals whose addresses are taken (forbidden &local coercion), (b) write-only frame arrays (forbidden dead-vars-local-array), (c) volatile coercion (forbidden), (d) macro-hidden __asm__ (forbidden), or (e) struct returns whose ABI fills caller stack (ruled out by caller use). The function legitimately stays INCOMPLETE at floor 27 per [[no-new-park-categories]] standing posture. CONCRETE NEXT ACTION (NOT a policy carve-out request): (a) build a directed PERM_GENERAL{} workspace with explicit u8/u16/u32/s32 type alternates per-temp AND explicit shift-count alternates (0x0/5/0xA/0xF) for a 4-6 hour overnight run — exhaustive directed search of the local mutation space the random pass may have under-sampled; (b) if that finds no legitimate sub-baseline, the residual is genuinely unreachable in pure C and the function joins the long-term INCOMPLETE pool with cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec / saEft00Add (the documented allocno-priority-tie / cross-jump-merge cluster — see [[register-alloc-pure-c]] § confirmed limits). Src reverted; oracle green at session end (verify-oracle ok:true, SHA1 == 62efab4f73f992798c43e8c730aa43baa10bb4fa).

sessions.lever: Round 8 — applied tmp/sotn_research_func_8007C97C_v2.md NOVEL levers: block-local OR-pack split (Lever A variants), struct-typed arg0, u32 temps with (u8) casts, separate s16 halfword reads, load reorder, early-return with top-level locals
sessions.note: FLOOR UNCHANGED at 27. Applied round-7 candidate (u8-typed r/g/b1/b2, score 27 confirmed) and tested 6 NEW structural variants from v2 research, NONE of which appear in prior sessions' rejected_forms: V8 hi/lo block-local split (`u32 hi = (b1<<0xF)|(r<<0xA); u32 lo = (b2<<5)|g; return hi|0xE2000000u|lo;`) = score 29 (REGRESSED +2, build_insns=24). V9 in-place accumulator (`u32 acc = (r<<0xA)|0xE2000000u; acc |= b1<<0xF; acc |= b2<<5; acc |= g;`) = score 29 (REGRESSED +2, build_insns=25). V10 struct-typed arg0 (`TexWin {u8 mx; u8 ox; u8 _pad2; u8 _pad3; s16 my; s16 oy;}`) = score 27 (SAME — typing change has no allocation effect because parameter is just dereferenced, struct doesn't change loads). V11 u32 temps with explicit (u8) inner casts (`u32 r = (u32)arg0[0]>>3; u32 g = (u32)((u8)((-...)>>3));`) = score 29 (REGRESSED +2). V12 load reorder r→b1→g→b2 (Lever A variant — declare temps then assign in target's temporal order) = score 27 (SAME). V13 separate s16 halfword reads (`s16 mx = *(s16*)(arg0+4); s16 my = *(s16*)(arg0+6); u8 g = (-mx)>>3;`) = score 27 (SAME — extra named locals get folded back). V14 early-return with top-level locals (`u8 r,g,b1,b2; if (arg0==0) return 0; r = ...;`) = score 28 (REGRESSED +1, build_insns=26 — early-return form adds 2 insns vs body-internal if). Conclusion: Every novel structural variant outside the round-7 winning shape either holds at 27 or regresses. The round-7 candidate (u8 temps + body-internal if + single OR-pack expression with explicit u32 casts) IS the floor; nothing else in the explored search space drops below it WITHOUT a coercion. The v2 research's struct-return hypothesis was ruled out by inspecting callers (`initClearImage`/`initTexPage`/`func_8007C2A0` all use the return as `*(u32*)` — the function must return s32/u32, not a struct, so the hidden-stack-pointer hypothesis doesn't apply). Concrete next-attempt avenues genuinely remaining: (a) directed permuter with PERM_INT_TYPE on residual variables (v2 lever #1 — un-run this session due to budget); (b) GCC -da dump to read allocno priorities at the 27-floor candidate's RTL (v2 lever #4, un-run); (c) reorg.c-level investigation: the missing 2 nops are scheduling artifacts of the 4 dead stores; understanding what RTL structure cc1's reorg.c WOULD treat as needing those nops could expose a lever. Src reverted; oracle green at session end (verify-oracle ok:true, SHA1 == oracle). Per [[no-new-park-categories]] PARK_CANDIDATE is NOT terminal for this; the function continues to grind toward COMPLETED-C in future sessions.

sessions.lever: Round 7 — narrow integer type (Lever B from .claude/rules/register-alloc-pure-c.md) on color channel temps (r/g/b1/b2 as u8) + 12-min/~6749-iter permuter from round-5 candidate base
sessions.note: FLOOR LOWERED 29 → 27 (2-point improvement, build_insns 25 → 24). The lever: type the four color-channel temps as u8 (their natural width — color channels are 0..255 by domain) instead of u32/s32; explicit u32 casts in the final OR-pack expression. This is Lever B from [[register-alloc-pure-c]] — narrow integer typing is a documented legitimate technique (sibling rules [[narrow-byte-args-packed-call]], [[narrow-stack-param-subword-offset]]). NOT a cheat: u8 is the natural type for color channels (a programmer writing this from spec would naturally use u8), not reverse-engineered coercion; no padding, no address-of, no dead stores; the type narrowing produces real semantic information about value ranges. Variant ledger (all measured against round-5 candidate at floor 29): V1 halfword-pointer alias `s16 *hp = (s16*)(arg0+4)` = 29 (no change); V2 in-place OR accumulator `ret |= ...` = 30 (regression); V3 u8-typed temps + explicit u32 casts = 27 (winner); V4 simplified V3 (no inner u8 casts) = 27 (same score, same build_insns 24); V5 V3 + named e2 intermediate = 27 (build_insns 25, slightly different masking). V3 saved as new candidate.c. Permuter (12 min wallclock, ~6749 iters, 6 workers) saved ~700 outputs; best legitimate-shape weighted score 1010 in output-1010-1 — uses `s32 *new_var = &temp_v1;` address-coercion construct producing dead `sw` stores via &local materialization. REJECT: same family as round-0 sp[4] cheat per [[no-new-park-categories]] cheats-by-any-spelling + [[dead-vars-local-array]] scalar-address-coercion. Saved at rejected/addr_coerce_temp.c. Outputs 1095-1 (uses `u32 *new_var2 = &temp_v0;`), 1205-1, 1320-1 — all same address-coercion family. The permuter's PERM_GENERAL random mutation will keep finding this; ignore them all. Src reverted; oracle green at session end (verify-oracle ok:true). The function remains INCOMPLETE at floor 27; the 9-insn remaining gap is still structurally the dead stack writes + frame slop, which sessions 4-6's mechanism analysis established cannot be reached via any legitimate pure-C construct.

sessions.lever: SOTN-precedent research (tmp/sotn_research_func_8007C97C.md, 2026-06-04) — DEFINITIVE NEGATIVE RESULT on widening canonical-asm gate via SOTN precedent
sessions.note: Fresh SOTN-decomp research agent (sources: github.com/Xeeynamo/sotn-decomp STYLE.md, README.md, wiki, include/include_asm.h, src/st/, src/main_psp/) confirms three findings that close the standing canonical-asm policy question for this function: (1) SOTN has NO documented policy on 'dead stack writes the C didn't ask for' — zero hits across docs/wiki/issues for 'dead store', 'dead stack', 'frame coercion', 'unused frame', 'stack write'. (2) SOTN's escape hatch `INCLUDE_ASM(FOLDER, NAME)` from include/include_asm.h auto-suffixes every symbol `.NON_MATCHING` and lives under `nonmatchings/` paths — this is the SOTN equivalent of BB2's PARK state ('not yet matched, may revisit'), NOT the equivalent of BB2's COMPLETED-INLINE-ASM-CANONICAL tier. There is NO SOTN analog of BB2's curated inline_asm_canonical.txt list with positive S1/S2/S6/S8 criteria. (3) SOTN's matched corpus has NO GP0 builder with this shape (4 dead stack writes the C didn't ask for); searches of SOTN code for `addiu $sp` in C source returned only one hit — a comment in tools/make-config.py about prologue detection — confirming SOTN has no inline-asm prologue-spillage patterns in matched src/. DIRECT POLICY ANSWER: under SOTN precedent, func_8007C97C would sit as `INCLUDE_ASM('asm/.../nonmatchings/...', func_8007C97C)` indefinitely, counted as 'not yet matched'. Under BB2's stricter two-tier policy (PARK ≡ SOTN INCLUDE_ASM; COMPLETED-INLINE-ASM-CANONICAL = BB2-specific with no SOTN analog), the SOTN-precedent honest categorization is PARK — its current state. This CONFIRMS the standing canonical-asm rejection from session 1-5 and the notes.md TOP-OF-FILE policy: absence of pure-C path is not the canonical-asm threshold; positive S1/S2/S6/S8 signals are, and SOTN provides no precedent for widening the gate to accept 'no pure-C form possible after N sessions' as a sufficient condition. Sibling evidence from session 5 (func_8007C8AC GP0(0xE4) and func_8007C938 GP0(0xE5) are clean pure C with zero dead stack writes despite identical OR-pack idiom) is consistent with the SOTN-corpus null result: nothing in SOTN's matched code has this shape either. No new lever; no candidate.c modification; src untouched; oracle green at session end (HEAD 88b90686). The function legitimately stays PARKED per SOTN precedent; no further iteration on canonical-asm authorization is expected to produce a different answer.

sessions.lever: Round 11 — two NEW micro-variants on the round-7 u8 candidate base (NOT previously tested per rejected_forms / sessions 1-10): V_andi_before_shift (explicit (u8) cast BEFORE the >>3, forcing andi-before-shift ordering in cc1's RTL — distinct from round 8 V11 which cast AFTER the shift); V_named_packed (separate `packed_r`/`packed_b1`/`packed_b2` u32 locals materializing each shift result before the OR-pack)
sessions.note: FLOOR UNCHANGED at 27. Confirmed candidate.c floor 27 first (target_insns=33, build_insns=24, rules_dropped=9, cheat_asm_stripped=446, scorable=true). Two NEW manual variants tested, neither previously in rejected_forms or any session's variant ledger: (1) V_andi_before_shift — replaced `(-X)>>3` with `(u8)(-X)>>3` for the s16 channels (g and b2). The intent: force cc1 to emit `negu, andi 0xFF, sra 3` ordering matching target's instruction sequence at lines 13-15 / 27-29 of asm/funcs/func_8007C97C.s. Distinct from round 8 V11 which cast AFTER the shift (`u32 g = (u32)((u8)((-X)>>3))`). Measured: score=27, build_insns=24 — SAME as baseline. The (u8) cast before the shift folds equivalently in combine; cc1 still chooses its own ordering. (2) V_named_packed — declared `u32 packed_r, packed_b1, packed_b2` and materialized each shifted-byte product into its own named local before the final OR. Intent: extend each shift result's RTL pseudo lifetime past the surrounding negu/andi/sra cluster so cc1 schedules the shifts earlier (closer to target's interleaved `sll $a1,$a1,10` mid-computation pattern). Measured: score=28, build_insns=24 — REGRESSED +1. The named-locals form adds an `move`-style RTL insn that doesn't fold and shifts the post-OR-pack register cadence. ADDITIONAL OBSERVATION worth recording: examined the target asm's spill timing carefully — the 4 `sw $rN, K($sp)` stores are NOT uniformly placed: sp[0] stores $a1 (r-shifted) BEFORE the `sll $a1,$a1,10` that overwrites it (lines 10-11), sp[4] stores $v0 (b1-shifted) BEFORE the `sll $v0,$v0,15` overwrite (lines 20-21), sp[8] stores $a2 (g-finalized) AFTER computation but $a2 is REUSED later as an OR operand (line 16 sw; line 31 or). Only sp[C] (b2-final) is a true tail store (line 32). This pattern — register spilled at the OVERWRITE BOUNDARY but the register simultaneously needed for the post-shift value — is the GCC 2.7.2 fingerprint of `-g -O2` debug-info-preserving codegen: cc1 with `-g` keeps DWARF location lists updated by ensuring every variable's CURRENT VALUE is observable in memory at every breakpoint-eligible instruction boundary, hence spilling each value BEFORE overwriting its register. Our build does NOT use `-g`; per [[no-compiler-divergence]] the toolchain flags are frozen (see [[compiler-flags-canonical]]'s closed-loop proof: every file holds many -O2 matches at our current flags, so the original BB2 build was also -O2 without `-g`). The dead-spill pattern in C97C target is therefore NOT explained by a debug-flag mismatch — but the spill-at-overwrite fingerprint matches a pattern that NORMALLY only -g produces, suggesting either (a) the original C97C function was hand-coded asm intentionally written to look like -g codegen (cf. [[hand-coded-asm-recognition]] S6 'custom ABI' — would need fresh signal audit), or (b) the original C used some toolchain trick we don't have a precedent for. Per the standing canonical-asm-rejection (notes.md TOP-OF-FILE) S1-S8 signals remain absent in target.s (no /* handwritten */, no hardcoded source regs, standard ABI, no GTE/BIOS/scratchpad, no packed-multiply low-bit-mask tell); the debug-style spill fingerprint alone is NOT a recognized canonical-asm signal. So this observation is mechanism-explanatory but not a policy lever. CUMULATIVE EXHAUSTION LEDGER (after 11 sessions): 32+ structural variants tested by hand, 19000+ permuter iterations across rounds 7+10 (PERM_GENERAL random), GCC -da RTL dump confirming minimal scratch-reg codegen, sibling cross-reference (8007C8AC/8007C938 both pure-C with zero dead stores), SOTN-precedent research (definitive negative), instrumented mechanism analysis (DCE removes any local not read), index-aligned objdump diff of stripped candidate vs target, debug-style spill fingerprint analysis (this round). The 4-dead-sw-stores residual is structurally not C-reachable through any tested or theoretically-derivable construct. Src reverted; oracle green at session end (verify-oracle ok:true, SHA1 == 62efab4f73f992798c43e8c730aa43baa10bb4fa, build_matches:true). CONCRETE NEXT ACTION (carry-through, NOT a policy carve-out request): (a) hand-authored PERM_GENERAL{...} macro embedding in base.c, with explicit non-coercion structural alternatives per C-statement (per round 10's identified frontier — the random-mutation neighborhood produces only forbidden coercion constructs; designing legitimate-only mutations requires hand-authored directives covering the OR-pack ordering, per-channel computation grouping, and parameter-type axes the rounds 8-11 manual variants did not span); (b) cross-reference with Lightweight's Kengo PS2 codebase if accessible — Kengo (PS2, 2001) reuses the same Marionation engine; the analogous GP0 color builder may shed light on the original C structure if Kengo source exists; (c) investigate if `display.c` is splat-attributed to the original BB2 TU correctly — if the GP0-builder cluster originally lived in a separate .c file with subtly different cc1 invocation context (e.g. surrounding function pressure changing RA), splat re-attribution could be the path, but requires evidence per [[no-new-park-categories]]'s evidence-based re-attribution guideline. The function legitimately stays INCOMPLETE at floor 27 in queue.json under its current 9 rules + cheat-asm. Per [[no-new-park-categories]] PARK_CANDIDATE is NOT terminal.

sessions.lever: Round 10 — extended PERM_GENERAL permuter (~12k iters, 6 workers, 20 min wallclock) from the u8 round-7 base; GCC -da dump (.greg/.lreg/.combine/.sched) of the round-7 candidate to read RTL pseudo allocation; objdump alignment of sandbox-stripped candidate vs target to pinpoint the exact 9-insn gap
sessions.note: FLOOR UNCHANGED at 27. Re-applied round-7 candidate (u8 r/g/b1/b2 + (u32)cast OR pack), sandbox confirmed score 27 (target_insns=33, build_insns=24, rules_dropped=9, cheat_asm_stripped=446, scorable=true). Two NEW concrete investigations executed this round, both adding empirical evidence that the 9-insn structural gap is unreachable by any non-cheat pure-C: (1) GCC -da dump on the round-7 candidate (tmp/c97c_dump/base.c.greg + .lreg + .combine + .sched) reveals the candidate uses ZERO callee-save regs — only $v0/$v1/$a0/$a1. The 24-insn body allocates within 4 scratch regs. cc1's natural codegen has no reason to spill to frame because (a) no function calls, (b) all live ranges fit in scratch regs, (c) no locals taken-address. To MATCH target's 33 insns with the 4 dead `sw $rN, K($sp)` stores at sp[0]/sp[4]/sp[8]/sp[C] + frame alloc/dealloc + 2 nops, GCC would need to be forced into using callee-save regs OR spilling to frame — and no pure-C construct accomplishes either without a forbidden coercion. (2) Index-aligned objdump of the candidate's stripped output vs target.s confirms the exact gap: candidate's early-NULL-return compresses to `bnez/move-v0,zero(delay)/jr` (3 insns of NULL handling), while target uses `bnez/addiu(sp,-0x10)(delay)/j END/addu(v0,0,0)` (4 insns) + 4 dead sw stores after computation + 2 nops + epilogue addiu(sp,0x10). The mandatory frame allocation EVEN ON THE NULL PATH is the structural signature of target — no pure-C compiles to that pattern without a stack-using construct. (3) Permuter run from u8 base — 12000+ iters / 6 workers / 20 min wallclock / base score 3065 weighted / --best-only mode / all output written to permuter/func_8007C97C/output-*. BEST FIVE legitimate-shape finds: weighted 1625, 1790, 1800, 1850, 1915. ALL FIVE are cheats by spelling: output-1625-1 (`u8 *new_var2 = &b1;` — scalar-address coercion, [[dead-vars-local-array]] / [[inline-asm-injection]] family); output-1790-1 (`volatile int b2 = ...` + `if(1){} if(1){}...` — volatile coercion + sanctioned-only-narrowly do-while-zero analogue); output-1800-1 (chained `& 0xFF & 0xFF & 0xFF...` bit-noise GCC eats — no semantic purpose, falls into the cheats-by-any-spelling NO-semantic-purpose test #2 of [[no-new-park-categories]]); output-1850-1 (`volatile int r` + `if (1)` wrap); output-1915-1+2 (volatile coercion variants). ZERO sub-baseline LEGITIMATE candidates in 12000+ iters. (4) Consistent with sessions 5-9 ledger: the PERM_GENERAL random-mutation neighborhood from the round-7 u8 base produces the SAME family of forbidden coercion constructs (volatile, address-of, dead-store) that the prior 6749-iter round-7 run produced from the wider base. The local-mutation search space is exhausted; randomized mutation does not synthesize a non-coercion structural lever capable of producing target's 4 dead stack writes. (5) The empirical exhaustion ledger now spans 10 sessions, 30+ structural variants tested by hand, 19000+ permuter iterations (round 7's 6749 + round 10's 12000+), GCC -da RTL dump on the candidate, sibling cross-reference (func_8007C8AC GP0(0xE4)/func_8007C938 GP0(0xE5) both pure-C with zero dead stores), SOTN-precedent research (round 6, definitive negative result), instrumented mechanism analysis (DCE removes any local not read; 4 stack stores require &local / volatile / write-only-array / struct-return — all forbidden or ABI-ruled-out by caller analysis). The 4-dead-sw-stores residual is structurally not C-reachable. Src reverted; oracle green at session end (verify-oracle ok:true, SHA1 == 62efab4f73f992798c43e8c730aa43baa10bb4fa). CONCRETE NEXT ACTION (NOT a policy carve-out request): the remaining un-exhausted avenue is a directed-PERM_GENERAL{...} workspace with explicit semantically-equivalent code snippet alternatives PER C-statement (vs the default random alternative-snippets pass which produces no-purpose mutations) — this would require hand-authored `PERM_GENERAL(a, b, c)` macro embeddings in base.c covering specifically the OR-pack expression and the per-channel masking. The challenge: the round 7 + round 10 finding is that ANY mutation that successfully lowers the weighted score introduces a forbidden construct. Designing PERM_GENERAL alternatives that explore non-coercion structural space is the actual remaining frontier; it is constructive work for a future round.

sessions.lever: round 12 SOTN-get_tw ch[4] non-sequential write order — borderline-research DO-NOT-SANCTION
sessions.note: Tested SOTN libgpu/sys.c get_tw matched form (u_long pad[4] -> ch[4] with non-sequential write order [0],[2],[1],[3], all slots read in return). EMPIRICAL: floor 27 -> 15 (build_insns 24 -> 31, target 33, -2 from match). Sequential write order regressed to 23, confirming non-sequential is load-bearing. Cheat-reviewer issued NEEDS_USER on the non-sequential write order Test #3 (GCC-internals justification borderline). User requested formal 2026-06-02-style borderline-research methodology survey. Research outcome (tmp/borderline_research_array_nonseq_2026-06-05.md): DO-NOT-SANCTION: only 1 SOTN matched instance (get_tw); siblings get_cs/ce/ofs do NOT use pattern; ZERO PR/issue/wiki/comment artifact; structural family already forbidden via dead-vars-local-array thin variant; closest sanctioned techniques sanction semantically meaningful facts (this is write-order on scratch buffer with no semantic content); large abuse surface (N/order/naming-laundering). Form saved at rejected/ch4_nonseq_sotn_get_tw.c. Floor remains 27 per no-new-park-categories standing posture. Honest pure-C ceiling unchanged.

reviewer.evidence: Round-7 candidate (inverted-null-check + u8 color-channel temps + u32-cast OR pack) passes the 6-test review checklist: (1) Semantic purpose: u8 IS the natural type for color channels (0-255 by domain) — narrowing is a meaningful constraint, not a coercion artifact. (2) Human-programmer test: a programmer writing a GP0 color-packet builder from spec would naturally type RGB components as u8. (3) GCC-internals justification test: the lever's justification is `Lever B narrow integer type per [[register-alloc-pure-c]]` — that rule documents narrow types as a legitimate codegen lever (not a GCC-internals coercion); siblings [[narrow-byte-args-packed-call]], [[narrow-stack-param-subword-offset]] confirm the pattern. (4) Permuter-found test: this lever was hand-derived from the catalog, not permuter-found. The permuter's wins (1010 etc) were correctly REJECTED as address-coercion cheats and saved at rejected/addr_coerce_temp.c. (5) Family check: no constructs from the cheat catalog (no pins, no __asm__, no padding, no &local, no volatile coercion, no dead stores, no scheduling barriers). (6) Naming-announces-intent test: variables named r, g, b1, b2 announce semantic purpose (RGB color channels), not coercion intent. Prior round-0 sp[4] FAIL remains valid for that form; the round-7 candidate is a separate, lower-floor LEGITIMATE base.

remaining_gap.summary: The 9-insn gap is structurally pinned to: (a) target's mandatory frame allocation even on NULL path (4 insn: bnez/addiu-sp(delay)/j-END/addu-v0,0,0), (b) 4 dead `sw $rN, K($sp)` stores at sp[0/4/8/12] after computation, (c) 2 reorg.c-emitted nops, (d) epilogue addiu sp,0x10. The candidate uses ZERO callee-save regs (per round-10 GCC -da dump — only $v0/$v1/$a0/$a1) and has no live-range pressure that would force frame allocation. cc1's DCE removes any pure-C local that is written but never read; no pure-C construct produces dead stack writes that survive DCE without being itself a cheat-by-spelling. After 10 sessions / 19000+ permuter iters / 30+ structural variants / instrumented mechanism analysis / sibling cross-reference / SOTN-precedent research, the round-7 floor 27 is the lowest non-cheat measurement found. The function remains INCOMPLETE; continued search for a non-coercion structural lever is the standing posture per [[no-new-park-categories]] (PARK_CANDIDATE is NOT terminal for this function).
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: Same 9 regfix substs as HEAD (regfix.txt â€” they were dropped by retire in 0e845d2, but restored by the revert). New shape may need different rules; un-knowable without measurement.

next_hypotheses: BORDERLINE-RESEARCH FINDING 2026-06-05 — the SOTN get_tw ch[4] non-sequential write order form (empirically score 15, lowest legitimate-shape floor across 12 sessions) investigated via formal 2026-06-02-style methodology and ruled out: only 1 SOTN matched instance, no sibling support, no PR/wiki/code-review artifact, structural family already forbidden via dead-vars-local-array thin variant. Form preserved at rejected/ch4_nonseq_sotn_get_tw.c. Honest pure-C floor remains 27. Per [[no-new-park-categories]] PARK_CANDIDATE not terminal; function remains INCOMPLETE-active.

PARK_CANDIDATE ESCALATION (session 5, 2026-06-04) — USER POLICY JUDGMENT REQUIRED. After 5 sessions of focused work, the empirical record is now strong enough to escalate the standing canonical-asm-rejection. The decision needed: should func_8007C97C be reclassified as COMPLETED-INLINE-ASM-CANONICAL (added to inline_asm_canonical.txt) despite lacking strong S1/S2/S6/S8 signals per .claude/rules/hand-coded-asm-recognition.md? EVIDENCE FOR escalation: (a) Honest pure-C floor = 29 measured/re-measured across 2 sessions (round 4 + 5). (b) The 8-insn gap is exclusively 4 dead `sw $rN, K($sp)` stores at sp[0]/sp[4]/sp[8]/sp[C] (matching a 4-element struct or array layout) + 0x10 frame alloc/dealloc + 2 nops. (c) GCC's DCE eliminates any pure-C local that is written but never read; no pure-C construct emits dead stack writes without being itself a cheat-by-spelling (write-only array per [[dead-vars-local-array]], `(void)&local;` address-coercion, dead self-assignment, volatile-coercion cast, struct with dead fields — all forbidden per [[no-new-park-categories]] cheats-by-any-spelling). (d) Two structurally-analogous siblings in the SAME FILE (func_8007C8AC GP0(0xE4), func_8007C938 GP0(0xE5)) build other GP0 command words via the same OR-pack idiom in pure C with ZERO dead stack writes — 8007C97C is anomalous within its own family. (e) Permuter from candidate base is not viable because no permuter mutation produces dead stack writes that survive DCE. EVIDENCE AGAINST escalation: hand-coded-asm-recognition's STRONG-tier requires S1/S2/S6/S8 positive signals (handwritten-instruction comments in target.s = ABSENT; hardcoded source-register signatures = ABSENT; custom-ABI / non-standard arg passing = ABSENT; packed-multiply low-bits-mask tell = ABSENT). Target asm has no GTE/BIOS/scratchpad/no-C-form constructs and uses standard $a0..$a3 ABI. The standing rejection's logic: 'absence of pure-C path' is not the same as 'positive hand-coded signals,' and the latter is what the canonical-asm rule requires. THE QUESTION FOR THE USER: should the canonical-asm gate be widened to accept 'no pure-C form possible after N sessions of evidence' as a sufficient condition, OR is the gate's 'positive hand-coded signals' bar a true hard constraint that means func_8007C97C must remain INCOMPLETE indefinitely? A precedent decision (saEft00Add / cpu_side_move_dir_4 cluster, see [[cross-jump-store-tail-merge]] § residual-6 / [[register-alloc-pure-c]] § marionation_Exec) leaves those functions parked INCOMPLETE with cheats; func_8007C97C would follow the same pattern unless the gate is widened. If the user policy is to widen the gate, the legitimate finished form is: file-scope `__asm__("glabel func_8007C97C; ...")` block matching target bytes, listed in inline_asm_canonical.txt, with all 9 regfix rules + the cheat-asm pins removed from src/display.c. If the policy remains as-is, the function stays INCOMPLETE in queue.json under its current 9 rules + cheat-asm + sp[4] coercion in src.

Workflow round 3 (2026-06-03) blocker — sandbox `func_8007C97C --disable all` is non-scorable on display.c due to the display.c-wide cheat-asm-strip pipeline truncation. The C86C round-3 worker's workaround `--keep-cheat-asm` flag is the unblocker IF func_8007C97C itself has no cheat-asm in its body (likely true — verify). Once the baseline is measurable, the FIRST step remains as below.

FIRST: measure the candidate. Apply candidate.c (the inverted-null-check + m2c body without sp[4]) to src/display.c, run `& tools/eng.ps1 sandbox func_8007C97C --disable all` (try with `--keep-cheat-asm` if base fails). Record candidate_floor in meta.json. The score-null state is the round-1 worker's measurement failure (worktree toolchain not built); a worker with a built toolchain measures it in seconds.

Apply candidate.c, then directed permuter from the measured base with PER-CANDIDATE cheat-reviewer gating. The rejected form (sp[4] write-only array) demonstrated the engine's mechanical detector gap; any permuter output MUST be vetted against the catalog AND independently reviewed before commit.

ALLOCDBG-instrumented dump on the candidate to identify what's blocking the natural frame layout. The legitimate base may have a register-allocation tiebreaker the inverted-null-check structure exposes.
Narrow integer typing exploration — s16/u16/u8 on temp_a1/a2/v0/v1 may shift cc1's codegen to match target's frame without coercion. Tested in isolation; record the floor for each.
Write-only-array detector closure (engine/volatile_cheats.py) — separate work item from the revert commit. Mechanically prevents future sp[N] / pad[N] / spill[N] re-derivation under different names.

rejected_forms.reason: REJECTED. Round 1 worker (and prior sessions) periodically propose this when the legitimate pure-C lever search plateaus. It is NOT evidence-backed for this function. Per .claude/rules/hand-coded-asm-recognition.md the strict gate requires STRONG-tier signals: S1 (/* handwritten instruction */ annotations in target.s) = 0; S2 (hardcoded source register signatures, SOTN tell) = none; S6 (custom-ABI / non-standard arg passing) = none; S8 (redundant low-bits mask before discarding shift, packed-multiply tell) = no. Target asm has zero no-C-form constructs (no GTE cop2 ops, no BIOS trampolines, no scratchpad pokes, no hand-scheduled GTE latency interleaving). Every instruction has a direct C analog. Per .claude/rules/no-compiler-divergence.md the toolchain is frozen; per .claude/rules/difficult-is-not-impossible.md a stuck function is unfinished work, never proven-impossible. Standing user policy: any future worker proposing canonical-asm for this function MUST first surface a fresh measured S1/S2/S6/S8 audit demonstrating STRONG-tier evidence — the absence-of-such-evidence is itself the answer, not a 'maybe ask the user' escalation.
